summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #552 in the moltis repo: the initial provider setup model selector only allowed single-model selection, unlike the existing multi-select modal used for already-configured providers.
summary.outcome: Converted showModelSelector() to a multi-select flow with toggle selection, Select All/Deselect All, a status counter, and an explicit Continue button. Updated saveAndFinishProvider() to accept arrays of model IDs and save via the plural RPC. Single file change, no backend modifications needed.

repo: A multi-select modal (showMultiModelSelector) already existed for the 'Preferred Models' button on configured providers — the initial setup simply didn't reuse it.
The backend already supported multiple models via providers.save_models RPC and ProviderConfig.models: Vec<String>, so no Rust changes were needed.
normalize_model_list() handles deduplication of model lists on the backend.
Tailwind classes used in the new UI (shrink-0, min-h-0, flex-1) were already present in the compiled CSS because showMultiModelSelector already used them — no CSS rebuild required.
The onboarding view has its own separate model selector flow, not the showModelSelector function in providers.js.
The regex /Select Model/i in oauth E2E tests still matches 'Select Models' as a substring, so no test changes were needed.

code.finding: showModelSelector() — original single-select: clicking a card immediately called saveAndFinishProvider(). Converted to multi-select toggle with explicit Continue button.
saveAndFinishProvider() — originally accepted a single selectedModelId string. Refactored to accept an array; tests only the first model for connectivity, saves all via providers.save_models.
Existing caller passes null for BYOM providers — handled by the selectedModelIds ? [selectedModelIds] : [] fallback, preserving prior behavior.
showMultiModelSelector() — the proven multi-select pattern (toggle, Select All, status text, explicit Save) that was replicated in showModelSelector().

workflow: Biome was used for JS lint/format checks after edits.
Beads (internal issue tracker backed by Dolt) was used to create and claim a tracking issue linked to the GitHub issue.
The worktree pattern (/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/unmarred-hawthorn) is used for isolated feature work.

friction: Beads/Dolt server was down at the start of implementation, requiring multiple retries and manual server restart before issue creation succeeded.
Circuit breaker cooldown added delay to beads issue creation attempts.
open_items: E2E tests cover the single-model selection flow (onboarding-openai.spec.js, oauth.spec.js) but there are no tests specifically covering multi-model selection during initial provider setup — new tests could be added.
Success message says 'configured successfully with N models!' — consider whether UX copy needs review for the single-model case (shows '1 models').
